title: Nothing
description: The abstract world. No sky, no ground, no levels, no menus. Jobs reduce to their essential truths: Resolve, Faith, Logic, Edge, Voice.

lines: Nothing is heavier than you'd think.
Not darkness. Not emptiness. This was nothing. The space that exists before the concept of space is invented.
Their stats were gone. Not zeroed — gone. Their inventories existed in theory but had no interface. Their abilities were memories of abilities.

lines.text: The Firewall was Resolve. Had always been Resolve. Every stand, every block — not the armor, not the rules. Just the willingness to remain standing.
The Antivirus was Faith. The conviction that the person being healed was worth healing. Not certainty. Faith.
The Compiler was Logic. Taking what was known, applying what was true, and deriving what must follow. Also, he understood now, a kind of love.
The Exploit was Edge. Every system has a boundary. Every rule has a limit case. The edge of a thing is where you learn what the thing actually is.
The Broadcaster was Voice. Giving voice to what others couldn't express. The world exists more fully when it is spoken.

lines: They were, for the first time in five worlds, just people.
No levels. No menus. No rules.
